summary: The contract always launches exactly three method calls on one Foo instance, so correctness is a happens-before property over six launch permutations rather than a scalable runtime workload.

workload_bound.source_constraint: one thread calls each of first, second, and third exactly once

replacement_checks.evidence: Each method performs one callback and a constant number of synchronization operations.
All six source-valid launch permutations are authored and executed through the isolated concurrency runner.
The runner accepts only the callback sequence firstsecondthird for every launch order.
Every execution runs in an isolated subprocess with a hard timeout and daemon worker joins.
